mission.en: an important task or duty that a person or group is given to do

ex.en: The team completed their mission successfully.
ex.ko: 그 팀은 임무를 성공적으로 완수했다.

mission.en: a group of people sent to another country to do a particular job

ex.en: A trade mission visited the region last month.
ex.ko: 지난달 무역 사절단이 그 지역을 방문했다.

mission.en: religious work done to spread a faith, especially in a foreign country

ex.en: She joined a mission to help build schools abroad.
ex.ko: 그녀는 해외에 학교를 짓는 선교 활동에 참여했다.

ex.en: The hills looked misty in the early morning.
ex.ko: 이른 아침 언덕은 안개가 자욱해 보였다.

misunderstanding.en: a situation in which someone does not understand something correctly

ex.en: There was a misunderstanding about the meeting time.
ex.ko: 회의 시간에 대한 오해가 있었다.

mixing bowl.en: a large bowl used for mixing ingredients together, especially when cooking

ex.en: She poured the flour into a mixing bowl.
ex.ko: 그녀는 밀가루를 믹싱볼에 부었다.

modal verb.en: an auxiliary verb such as 'can', 'must', or 'will' that expresses ability, possibility, or necessity

ex.en: 'Can' and 'should' are common modal verbs in English.
ex.ko: 'can'과 'should'는 영어에서 흔한 조동사이다.

modem.en: a device that connects a computer to the internet through a telephone or cable line

ex.en: He restarted the modem to fix the connection.
ex.ko: 그는 연결 문제를 해결하려고 모뎀을 재시작했다.

moderate.en: average in amount, quality, or degree; not extreme

ex.en: He does a moderate amount of exercise every day.
ex.ko: 그는 매일 적당한 양의 운동을 한다.

moderate.en: (of opinions or policies) not extreme, especially in politics

ex.en: She has moderate political views.
ex.ko: 그녀는 온건한 정치적 견해를 가지고 있다.

moderate.en: lead a discussion or debate and make sure it stays fair and organized

ex.en: She will moderate tonight's debate.
ex.ko: 그녀는 오늘 밤 토론의 사회를 볼 것이다.

modify.en: make small changes to something in order to improve it or make it more suitable

ex.en: They modified the design to make it safer.
ex.ko: 그들은 더 안전하게 만들려고 디자인을 수정했다.

modify.en: (grammar) describe or limit the meaning of another word

ex.en: Adjectives modify nouns.
ex.ko: 형용사는 명사를 수식한다.

moisture.en: small amounts of water present in the air, in a substance, or on a surface

ex.en: The plant needs moisture to grow.
ex.ko: 그 식물은 자라기 위해 수분이 필요하다.

molecule.en: the smallest part of a substance that has all the chemical properties of that substance

ex.en: Water molecules consist of hydrogen and oxygen.
ex.ko: 물 분자는 수소와 산소로 이루어져 있다.

monitor.en: a screen that shows information from a computer

ex.en: She bought a new monitor for her desk.
ex.ko: 그녀는 책상에 놓을 새 모니터를 샀다.

monitor.en: watch and check something over a period of time

ex.en: Doctors monitored her heart rate closely.
ex.ko: 의사들은 그녀의 심박수를 면밀히 관찰했다.

monk.en: a man who lives in a religious community and devotes his life to prayer and simple living

ex.en: The monk spent his days in prayer and silence.
ex.ko: 그 수도승은 기도와 침묵 속에서 하루하루를 보냈다.

monster.en: an imaginary frightening creature, often large and unnatural-looking

ex.en: The children were scared of the monster in the story.
ex.ko: 아이들은 이야기 속 괴물을 무서워했다.

ex.en: How could anyone treat a child like that? He's a monster.
ex.ko: 어떻게 아이를 그렇게 대할 수 있지? 그는 괴물 같은 인간이다.

monthly.en: happening or done once every month

ex.en: We have a monthly meeting on the first Monday.
ex.ko: 우리는 매달 첫째 월요일에 회의를 한다.

ex.en: The magazine is published monthly.
ex.ko: 그 잡지는 매달 발행된다.

monument.en: a structure built to remember a famous person or event

ex.en: They built a monument to honor the soldiers.
ex.ko: 그들은 군인들을 기리기 위해 기념비를 세웠다.

moral.en: relating to standards of right and wrong behavior

ex.en: She always tries to make the moral choice.
ex.ko: 그녀는 항상 도덕적인 선택을 하려고 노력한다.

moral.en: a lesson about right and wrong that a story teaches

ex.en: The moral of the story is to always be honest.
ex.ko: 그 이야기의 교훈은 항상 정직해야 한다는 것이다.

moreover.en: used to add extra information to what has just been said

ex.en: The plan is expensive; moreover, it will take years to complete.
ex.ko: 그 계획은 비용이 많이 든다. 게다가 완료하는 데 몇 년이 걸릴 것이다.

mosquito.en: a small flying insect that bites and sucks blood

ex.en: A mosquito bit me on the arm.
ex.ko: 모기가 내 팔을 물었다.

moss.en: a small green plant that grows in a thick layer on rocks, trees, or damp ground

ex.en: Moss covered the old stone wall.
ex.ko: 이끼가 오래된 돌담을 뒤덮었다.

motherland.en: the country where a person was born

ex.en: He returned to his motherland after many years abroad.
ex.ko: 그는 여러 해 외국 생활 후 조국으로 돌아왔다.

ex.en: He stood motionless, watching the deer.
ex.ko: 그는 미동도 없이 서서 사슴을 지켜보았다.

motivate.en: give someone a reason or desire to do something

ex.en: Good teachers know how to motivate their students.
ex.ko: 좋은 교사는 학생들에게 동기를 부여하는 법을 안다.

motivation.en: the reason or enthusiasm you have for doing something

ex.en: She lacks the motivation to study every day.
ex.ko: 그녀는 매일 공부할 동기가 부족하다.

motive.en: the reason that makes someone do something, especially one that isn't stated openly

ex.en: Police couldn't find a motive for the crime.
ex.ko: 경찰은 그 범죄의 동기를 찾지 못했다.

motor.en: a machine that changes power, such as electricity, into movement

ex.en: The washing machine's motor stopped working.
ex.ko: 세탁기 모터가 작동을 멈췄다.

motorcycle.en: a vehicle with two wheels powered by an engine

ex.en: He rides his motorcycle to work every day.
ex.ko: 그는 매일 오토바이를 타고 출근한다.

motto.cue: 삶이나 단체의 신념을 짧게 나타낸 말
motto.en: a short sentence that expresses a belief or purpose

ex.en: "Never give up" is my personal motto.
ex.ko: '절대 포기하지 마라'는 나의 좌우명이다.

mount.en: fix or attach something to a support or surface

ex.en: They mounted the camera on the wall.
ex.ko: 그들은 카메라를 벽에 설치했다.

mount.en: get on a horse, bicycle, or other vehicle in order to ride it

ex.en: She mounted her bike and rode off.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자전거에 올라타서 떠났다.

mount.en: used before the name of a mountain

ex.en: Mount Everest is the tallest mountain in the world.
ex.ko: 에베레스트 산은 세계에서 가장 높은 산이다.

mount.en: gradually increase in amount or intensity

ex.en: Pressure mounted on the company to respond.
ex.ko: 그 회사에 대응하라는 압박이 점점 커졌다.

ex.en: They watched the sunrise from the mountaintop.
ex.ko: 그들은 산꼭대기에서 일출을 지켜보았다.

mourn.en: feel or show deep sadness, especially because someone has died

ex.en: The whole town mourned the loss of their beloved teacher.
ex.ko: 온 마을이 사랑받던 선생님의 죽음을 애도했다.

ex.en: Her boots were covered in mud after the hike.
ex.ko: 하이킹을 하고 나서 그녀의 부츠는 진흙투성이가 되었다.

mule.en: an animal that is the offspring of a donkey and a horse, used to carry loads

ex.en: The farmer used a mule to carry supplies up the hill.
ex.ko: 농부는 노새를 이용해 언덕 위로 짐을 날랐다.

murderer.en: a person who has deliberately killed another person

ex.en: The murderer was caught by the police within a week.
ex.ko: 그 살인자는 일주일 만에 경찰에 붙잡혔다.

muscle.en: a piece of body tissue that can contract to produce movement

ex.en: He pulled a muscle while lifting weights.
ex.ko: 그는 역기를 들다가 근육을 다쳤다.

mustard.en: a yellow paste made from mustard seeds, eaten with food

ex.en: He put mustard on his hot dog.
ex.ko: 그는 핫도그에 머스터드를 뿌렸다.

mutual.en: shared or felt by two or more people towards each other

ex.en: They have mutual respect for each other.
ex.ko: 그들은 서로에 대한 상호 존중을 가지고 있다.

myth.en: a traditional story, especially about gods or heroes, that explains something in nature or culture

ex.en: The myth explains how the world was created.
ex.ko: 그 신화는 세상이 어떻게 창조되었는지 설명한다.

myth.en: a widely believed idea that is actually false

ex.en: It's a myth that we only use ten percent of our brains.
ex.ko: 우리가 뇌의 10퍼센트만 사용한다는 것은 잘못된 통념이다.

nail.en: the hard, thin layer covering the end of a finger or toe

ex.en: She painted her nails bright red.
ex.ko: 그녀는 손톱을 밝은 빨간색으로 칠했다.

nail.en: a thin pointed piece of metal that is hammered into materials to join them

ex.en: He hit the nail with a hammer.
ex.ko: 그는 망치로 못을 박았다.

nail.en: attach something using a nail or nails

ex.en: They nailed the boards together to build a fence.
ex.ko: 그들은 널빤지들을 못으로 박아 울타리를 만들었다.

ex.en: The baby ran naked across the lawn.
ex.ko: 아기가 벌거벗은 채로 잔디밭을 가로질러 뛰었다.

naked.en: without using a telescope, microscope, or other instrument to see

ex.en: You can see the comet with the naked eye tonight.
ex.ko: 오늘 밤엔 육안으로도 그 혜성을 볼 수 있다.

nap.en: a short sleep, especially during the day

ex.en: She took a nap after lunch.
ex.ko: 그녀는 점심을 먹고 낮잠을 잤다.

nap.en: sleep for a short period, especially during the day

ex.en: The baby napped for two hours.
ex.ko: 아기는 두 시간 동안 낮잠을 잤다.

narrate.en: tell a story or describe events, often for a film or program

ex.en: The documentary was narrated by a famous actor.
ex.ko: 그 다큐멘터리는 유명 배우가 내레이션을 맡았다.

narrative.en: a spoken or written account of connected events; a story

ex.en: The novel has a complex narrative.
ex.ko: 그 소설은 복잡한 이야기 구조를 가지고 있다.

narrative.en: in the form of a story

ex.en: She prefers narrative writing to essays.
ex.ko: 그녀는 논설문보다 서술체 글쓰기를 선호한다.

narrow.en: having a small width compared to length

ex.en: They walked down a narrow street.
ex.ko: 그들은 좁은 거리를 따라 걸었다.

narrow.en: limited in scope, or unwilling to consider new ideas

ex.en: He has a narrow view of what success means.
ex.ko: 그는 성공의 의미에 대해 편협한 시각을 가지고 있다.

narrow.en: make or become smaller in width or range

ex.en: The gap between the two teams narrowed.
ex.ko: 두 팀 사이의 격차가 좁혀졌다.

ex.en: That was a nasty thing to say to her.
ex.ko: 그건 그녀에게 못되게 한 말이었다.

ex.en: He had a nasty cut on his leg.
ex.ko: 그는 다리에 심한 상처를 입었다.

nationalist.en: a person who strongly supports the independence or interests of their own nation

ex.en: The nationalist party won the election.
ex.ko: 그 민족주의 정당이 선거에서 승리했다.

ex.en: Naturally, she was upset when she heard the news.
ex.ko: 당연히 그녀는 그 소식을 듣고 속상해했다.

naturally.en: in a way that seems normal and not forced

ex.en: Just relax and try to smile naturally for the photo.
ex.ko: 긴장 풀고 사진 찍을 때 자연스럽게 웃어봐.

navy blue.en: a very dark shade of blue

ex.en: He wore a navy blue suit to the interview.
ex.ko: 그는 면접에 짙은 남색 정장을 입고 갔다.

ex.en: We had lunch at a nearby restaurant.
ex.ko: 우리는 근처 식당에서 점심을 먹었다.

nearby.en: in a place not far away

ex.en: There is a park nearby.
ex.ko: 근처에 공원이 있다.

necklace.en: a piece of jewelry worn around the neck

ex.en: She wore a pearl necklace to the party.
ex.ko: 그녀는 파티에 진주 목걸이를 하고 갔다.

nectar.en: a sweet liquid produced by flowers that attracts bees and other insects

ex.en: Bees collect nectar from flowers to make honey.
ex.ko: 벌은 꿀을 만들기 위해 꽃에서 화밀을 모은다.

nectar.en: a thick fruit juice, especially undiluted

ex.en: She drank a glass of peach nectar.
ex.ko: 그녀는 복숭아 넥타 한 잔을 마셨다.

needle.en: a thin, sharp pointed tool used for sewing or giving injections

ex.en: The nurse used a needle to give the injection.
ex.ko: 간호사는 바늘로 주사를 놓았다.

needle.en: a thin pointer on a dial or gauge that shows a measurement

ex.en: The needle on the fuel gauge was near empty.
ex.ko: 연료 계기판 바늘이 거의 바닥을 가리키고 있었다.

negotiate.en: discuss something with someone in order to reach an agreement

ex.en: The two companies negotiated a new contract.
ex.ko: 그 두 회사는 새 계약을 협상했다.

negotiate.en: successfully deal with or find a way through a difficult path or obstacle

ex.en: The driver carefully negotiated the sharp bend.
ex.ko: 운전자는 급커브를 조심스럽게 통과했다.

negotiation.en: the process of discussing something in order to reach an agreement

ex.en: The negotiation lasted for several hours.
ex.ko: 그 협상은 몇 시간 동안 계속되었다.

neighborhood.en: the area of a town or city where you live

ex.en: We live in a quiet neighborhood.
ex.ko: 우리는 조용한 동네에 산다.

neighborhood.en: an amount that is close to a particular number (used with 'in the')

ex.en: The repair cost was in the neighborhood of $500.
ex.ko: 수리 비용은 대략 500달러쯤 들었다.

nephew.en: the son of your brother or sister

ex.en: My nephew just started middle school.
ex.ko: 내 조카는 이제 막 중학교에 들어갔다.

nervousness.en: the feeling of being worried or anxious

ex.en: She tried to hide her nervousness before the interview.
ex.ko: 그녀는 면접 전에 긴장을 감추려 애썼다.

ness.en: a piece of land that sticks out into the sea

ex.en: The lighthouse stands on a rocky ness.
ex.ko: 그 등대는 바위투성이 곶 위에 서 있다.

network.en: a group of people or organizations that are connected or that work together

ex.en: She built a strong professional network.
ex.ko: 그녀는 탄탄한 인맥을 구축했다.

network.en: a system of connected computers, cables, or communication lines

ex.en: The office network went down for an hour.
ex.ko: 사무실 네트워크가 한 시간 동안 다운되었다.

network.en: meet and talk with people in order to exchange information and make useful contacts

ex.en: He attended the conference to network with other professionals.
ex.ko: 그는 다른 전문가들과 인맥을 쌓기 위해 학회에 참석했다.

neutral.en: not supporting or helping either side in a disagreement, competition, or war

ex.en: Switzerland remained neutral during the war.
ex.ko: 스위스는 전쟁 중 중립을 유지했다.

neutral.cue: 자동차 기어가 동력 전달 안 되는 위치
neutral.en: the position of a vehicle's gears in which no power is sent to the wheels

ex.en: He put the car in neutral at the red light.
ex.ko: 그는 빨간 신호에서 차를 중립에 놓았다.

nevertheless.en: despite what has just been said

ex.en: It was raining; nevertheless, they went for a walk.
ex.ko: 비가 오고 있었다. 그럼에도 불구하고 그들은 산책을 나갔다.

ex.en: The newborn baby slept peacefully.
ex.ko: 갓 태어난 아기가 평화롭게 잠들어 있었다.

newborn.cue: 태어난 지 얼마 안 된 아기
newborn.en: a baby that has just been born

ex.en: The hospital has a special ward for newborns.
ex.ko: 그 병원에는 신생아를 위한 특별 병동이 있다.

newcomer.en: a person who has recently arrived somewhere or started a new activity

ex.en: As a newcomer, she didn't know anyone at the office.
ex.ko: 신입이라 그녀는 사무실에서 아무도 알지 못했다.

ex.en: They live in a newly built apartment.
ex.ko: 그들은 새로 지어진 아파트에 산다.

next door.en: in or to the house or building right beside

ex.en: My best friend lives next door.
ex.ko: 내 가장 친한 친구는 옆집에 산다.

next-door.en: living or situated in the nearest house or building

ex.en: Our next-door neighbor is very friendly.
ex.ko: 우리 옆집 이웃은 매우 친절하다.

nickname.en: an informal name used instead of someone's real name

ex.en: His nickname at school was "Tiger".
ex.ko: 학교에서 그의 별명은 '타이거'였다.

nickname.en: give someone or something a nickname

ex.en: Friends nicknamed him "the Professor" because he loved reading.
ex.ko: 친구들은 그가 독서를 좋아해서 그를 '교수님'이라고 별명 지어 불렀다.

niece.en: the daughter of your brother or sister

ex.en: My niece is turning five next month.
ex.ko: 내 조카는 다음 달에 다섯 살이 된다.

nightlife.en: entertainment and social activities available at night, such as clubs and bars

ex.en: The city is famous for its exciting nightlife.
ex.ko: 그 도시는 신나는 밤 문화로 유명하다.

nod.en: move your head down and up, especially to show agreement or greeting

ex.en: She nodded to show she understood.
ex.ko: 그녀는 이해했다는 뜻으로 고개를 끄덕였다.

nod.en: a movement of the head down and up, especially to show agreement

ex.en: He gave a quick nod and left the room.
ex.ko: 그는 짧게 고개를 끄덕이고 방을 나갔다.

nod.en: let your head fall forward because you are falling asleep

ex.en: He kept nodding off during the boring lecture.
ex.ko: 그는 지루한 강의 중에 계속 꾸벅꾸벅 졸았다.

noisily.en: in a way that makes a lot of noise

ex.en: The children ran noisily down the hallway.
ex.ko: 아이들은 시끄럽게 복도를 뛰어다녔다.

none.en: not any person or thing; not one

ex.en: None of the students knew the answer.
ex.ko: 학생들 중 아무도 답을 알지 못했다.

nonetheless.en: despite what has just been said

ex.en: The task was difficult; nonetheless, she finished it on time.
ex.ko: 그 일은 어려웠다. 그럼에도 불구하고 그녀는 제시간에 끝냈다.

nonsense.en: ideas, words, or behavior that are silly or make no sense

ex.en: Stop talking nonsense and focus on the problem.
ex.ko: 허튼소리 그만하고 문제에 집중해.

nor.cue: neither와 짝을 이루어 부정을 이어줄 때
nor.en: used to introduce a second negative statement after 'neither' or 'not'

ex.en: She doesn't like coffee, nor does she like tea.
ex.ko: 그녀는 커피를 좋아하지 않고, 차도 좋아하지 않는다.

ex.en: Normally, I wake up at seven.
ex.ko: 보통 나는 7시에 일어난다.

northeast.en: the direction that is between north and east

ex.en: The storm is moving to the northeast.
ex.ko: 폭풍이 북동쪽으로 이동하고 있다.

northeast.en: situated in or facing the northeast

ex.en: They live in the northeast part of the city.
ex.ko: 그들은 도시의 북동쪽 지역에 산다.

northeastern.en: situated in or towards the northeast

ex.en: The northeastern coast is popular with tourists.
ex.ko: 북동쪽 해안은 관광객들에게 인기가 많다.

northern.en: situated in, of, or facing the north

ex.en: Northern Europe has cold winters.
ex.ko: 북유럽은 겨울이 춥다.

northwest.en: the direction that is between north and west

ex.en: The wind is blowing from the northwest.
ex.ko: 바람이 북서쪽에서 불어오고 있다.

northwest.en: situated in or facing the northwest

ex.en: We drove through the northwest region.
ex.ko: 우리는 북서쪽 지역을 지나 운전했다.

northwestern.en: situated in or towards the northwest

ex.en: The northwestern part of the country gets more rain.
ex.ko: 그 나라의 북서쪽 지역은 비가 더 많이 온다.

ex.en: There was a noticeable improvement in her English.
ex.ko: 그녀의 영어 실력에 눈에 띄는 향상이 있었다.

noticeboard.en: a board on a wall where notices and information are displayed

ex.en: Check the noticeboard for the exam schedule.
ex.ko: 시험 일정을 확인하려면 게시판을 봐.

notion.en: an idea or belief about something

ex.en: He has a strange notion of what success means.
ex.ko: 그는 성공의 의미에 대해 이상한 생각을 가지고 있다.

ex.en: The city is notorious for its traffic jams.
ex.ko: 그 도시는 교통 체증으로 악명이 높다.

ex.en: She became a famous novelist after her first book.
ex.ko: 그녀는 첫 책을 낸 후 유명한 소설가가 되었다.

nowhere.en: not in, at, or to any place

ex.en: I could find my keys nowhere.
ex.ko: 나는 열쇠를 어디에서도 찾을 수 없었다.

nowhere.cue: 아무것도 없는 외딴 곳 (in the middle of nowhere)
nowhere.en: a place that is far from other places and seems empty or isolated

ex.en: Their cabin is in the middle of nowhere.
ex.ko: 그들의 오두막은 아주 외딴 곳에 있다.

nuclear.en: relating to the nucleus of an atom, or using the energy released by nuclear reactions

ex.en: The country is building a new nuclear power plant.
ex.ko: 그 나라는 새 원자력 발전소를 짓고 있다.

nucleus.en: the central part of a cell that contains most of its genetic material

ex.en: The nucleus controls the activities of the cell.
ex.ko: 핵은 세포의 활동을 조절한다.

nucleus.en: the central part of an atom, containing protons and neutrons

ex.en: Protons and neutrons are found in the nucleus of an atom.
ex.ko: 양성자와 중성자는 원자핵 안에 있다.

nucleus.en: the central or most important part of a group

ex.en: These five players form the nucleus of the team.
ex.ko: 이 다섯 명의 선수가 그 팀의 핵심을 이룬다.

ex.en: She has won numerous awards for her research.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자신의 연구로 수많은 상을 받았다.

nutrient.en: a substance that living things need to grow and stay healthy

ex.en: Vegetables are rich in essential nutrients.
ex.ko: 채소에는 필수 영양소가 풍부하다.

nutrition.en: the food that a person or animal eats, and the way it affects health

ex.en: Good nutrition is important for children's growth.
ex.ko: 좋은 영양 섭취는 아이들의 성장에 중요하다.

nutrition.en: the scientific study of food and how it affects health

ex.en: She is studying nutrition at university.
ex.ko: 그녀는 대학에서 영양학을 공부하고 있다.

nutritious.en: (of food) containing substances the body needs to be healthy

ex.en: Eating nutritious food keeps you energized.
ex.ko: 영양가 있는 음식을 먹으면 활력이 유지된다.

ex.en: Several residents objected to the new plan.
ex.ko: 몇몇 주민들이 그 새 계획에 반대했다.

object.en: a thing that can be seen and touched

ex.en: There was a strange object on the table.
ex.ko: 탁자 위에 이상한 물건이 있었다.

object.en: (grammar) a word or phrase that is affected by the action of a verb

ex.en: In "She read the book", "the book" is the object.
ex.ko: 'She read the book'에서 'the book'이 목적어이다.

objection.en: a reason or statement expressing disagreement with something

ex.en: He raised an objection to the new policy.
ex.ko: 그는 새 정책에 이의를 제기했다.

objective.en: based on facts rather than personal feelings or opinions

ex.en: Try to give an objective opinion about the situation.
ex.ko: 그 상황에 대해 객관적인 의견을 주려고 노력해봐.

objective.en: a goal that you are trying to achieve

ex.en: The main objective of the project is to reduce costs.
ex.ko: 그 프로젝트의 주요 목표는 비용 절감이다.

observation.en: the act of watching someone or something carefully

ex.en: The scientist recorded her observations of the animals' behavior.
ex.ko: 그 과학자는 동물들의 행동을 관찰한 내용을 기록했다.

observation.en: a comment based on something you have noticed

ex.en: He made an interesting observation about the results.
ex.ko: 그는 그 결과에 대해 흥미로운 소견을 밝혔다.

ex.en: Scientists observed the stars through a telescope.
ex.ko: 과학자들은 망원경으로 별을 관찰했다.

observe.en: obey or follow a law, rule, or custom

ex.en: The family observes the tradition every year.
ex.ko: 그 가족은 매년 그 전통을 지킨다.

observe.en: make a remark or comment about something

ex.en: "It's getting late," she observed.
ex.ko: "시간이 늦어지네요," 그녀가 말했다.

obstacle.en: something that makes it difficult to do something or go somewhere

ex.en: Lack of money was a major obstacle to her plans.
ex.ko: 돈 부족이 그녀의 계획에 큰 장애물이었다.

obtain.en: get or acquire something, especially through effort

ex.en: She obtained a degree in economics.
ex.ko: 그녀는 경제학 학위를 취득했다.

obvious.cue: 누가 봐도 쉽게 알 수 있는
obvious.en: easy to see, understand, or notice

ex.en: It was obvious that she was upset.
ex.ko: 그녀가 화가 났다는 것이 명백했다.

obviously.en: in a way that is easy to see or understand; clearly

ex.en: Obviously, he didn't do his homework.
ex.ko: 명백히 그는 숙제를 하지 않았다.

occasion.en: a particular time when something happens

ex.en: On this occasion, we'll make an exception.
ex.ko: 이번 경우에는 예외를 두겠다.

ex.en: Her wedding was a joyous occasion.
ex.ko: 그녀의 결혼식은 기쁜 행사였다.

ex.en: We enjoy an occasional trip to the beach.
ex.ko: 우리는 가끔 해변으로 여행을 가는 것을 즐긴다.

ex.en: I occasionally go for a run in the morning.
ex.ko: 나는 가끔 아침에 조깅을 한다.

occupy.en: take up space, time, or a position

ex.en: The sofa occupies most of the living room.
ex.ko: 그 소파가 거실 대부분을 차지한다.

occupy.en: live or stay in a place

ex.en: The family has occupied this house for decades.
ex.ko: 그 가족은 수십 년간 이 집에 거주해왔다.

occupy.en: take control of a place, especially by military force

ex.en: The army occupied the city for several months.
ex.ko: 그 군대는 몇 달 동안 그 도시를 점령했다.

ex.en: The accident occurred late at night.
ex.ko: 그 사고는 밤늦게 발생했다.

ex.en: It occurred to me that I had forgotten her birthday.
ex.ko: 그녀의 생일을 잊었다는 생각이 문득 떠올랐다.

ocean.en: a very large area of sea

ex.en: They sailed across the Atlantic Ocean.
ex.ko: 그들은 대서양을 가로질러 항해했다.

offensive.en: causing someone to feel upset, angry, or insulted

ex.en: His comment was extremely offensive to many people.
ex.ko: 그의 발언은 많은 사람들에게 매우 무례하게 느껴졌다.

offensive.en: used for or relating to attacking, especially in war or sports

ex.en: The team switched to an offensive strategy.
ex.ko: 그 팀은 공격적인 전략으로 전환했다.

offensive.en: a military attack, or a determined effort to achieve something

ex.en: The army launched a major offensive at dawn.
ex.ko: 그 군대는 새벽에 대규모 공세를 개시했다.

officially.en: in a way that is authorized or done according to official rules

ex.en: The new law was officially announced today.
ex.ko: 새 법이 오늘 공식적으로 발표되었다.

oily.en: containing or covered with oil or fat

ex.en: Fried food tends to be oily.
ex.ko: 튀긴 음식은 기름진 경향이 있다.

ex.en: He always wears old-fashioned suits.
ex.ko: 그는 항상 구식 정장을 입는다.

oneself.en: used to refer back to the subject when the subject is 'one' or people in general

ex.en: It's important to believe in oneself.
ex.ko: 자기 자신을 믿는 것이 중요하다.

ex.en: She walked onstage to receive her award.
ex.ko: 그녀는 상을 받으러 무대 위로 걸어 나갔다.

ex.en: The onstage chemistry between the actors was clear.
ex.ko: 배우들 사이 무대 위에서의 케미가 뚜렷했다.

onto.en: to a position on the surface of something

ex.en: The cat jumped onto the table.
ex.ko: 고양이가 탁자 위로 뛰어올랐다.

opening.en: a space or hole that something can pass through

ex.en: Light came through a small opening in the wall.
ex.ko: 벽에 난 작은 구멍으로 빛이 들어왔다.

opening.en: the start or first event of something, such as an exhibition or store

ex.en: We attended the opening of the new museum.
ex.ko: 우리는 새 박물관의 개관식에 참석했다.

opening.en: a job or position that is available

ex.en: There is an opening for a marketing manager.
ex.ko: 마케팅 매니저 자리에 공석이 있다.

operation.en: the way something, especially a machine or system, works

ex.en: The operation of the new machine is simple.
ex.ko: 그 새 기계의 작동법은 간단하다.

operation.en: a medical procedure in which a surgeon cuts into the body to treat an illness or injury

ex.en: She had an operation on her knee.
ex.ko: 그녀는 무릎 수술을 받았다.

operation.en: an organized activity involving many people, especially military or business

ex.en: The rescue operation lasted three days.
ex.ko: 그 구조 작전은 사흘간 계속되었다.

opposition.en: strong disagreement with something, or an act of disagreeing

ex.en: There was strong opposition to the new tax.
ex.ko: 새 세금에 대한 강한 반대가 있었다.

opposition.en: the political party that is not in power and criticizes the government

ex.en: The opposition criticized the government's new policy.
ex.ko: 야당은 정부의 새 정책을 비판했다.

oppress.en: treat people cruelly or unfairly, keeping them from having rights or freedom

ex.en: The government oppressed anyone who spoke out against it.
ex.ko: 그 정부는 반대 목소리를 내는 사람은 누구든 억압했다.

oppression.en: the unjust treatment of people, denying them rights and freedom

ex.en: They fought against years of oppression.
ex.ko: 그들은 수년간의 억압에 맞서 싸웠다.

option.cue: 고를 수 있는 여러 가지 중 하나
option.en: one of the things you can choose from

ex.en: We have several options for dinner tonight.
ex.ko: 우리는 오늘 저녁 식사로 몇 가지 선택지가 있다.

movement.en: the act of moving or changing position

ex.en: The sudden movement startled the cat.
ex.ko: 갑작스러운 움직임에 고양이가 놀랐다.

movement.en: an organized effort by a group of people to achieve a social or political goal

ex.en: She was active in the civil rights movement.
ex.ko: 그녀는 시민권 운동에 적극적으로 참여했다.

movement.en: one of the main sections of a long piece of music such as a symphony

ex.en: The symphony has four movements.
ex.ko: 그 교향곡은 4개의 악장으로 이루어져 있다.

ex.en: The teacher gave an oral exam at the end of the course.
ex.ko: 선생님은 과정이 끝날 때 구술 시험을 봤다.

oral.en: relating to or taken through the mouth

ex.en: Brush your teeth regularly for good oral hygiene.
ex.ko: 좋은 구강 위생을 위해 이를 규칙적으로 닦아라.

orbit.en: the curved path that a planet, moon, or satellite follows around another object in space

ex.en: The satellite is in orbit around the Earth.
ex.ko: 그 위성은 지구 궤도를 돌고 있다.

orbit.en: move around an object in space in a fixed path

ex.en: The moon orbits the Earth.
ex.ko: 달은 지구 주위를 돈다.

orchestra.cue: 여러 악기가 함께 연주하는 큰 악단
orchestra.en: a large group of musicians who play different instruments together

ex.en: The orchestra performed a beautiful symphony.
ex.ko: 그 오케스트라는 아름다운 교향곡을 연주했다.

orderly.en: well organized, or behaving in a calm and controlled way

ex.en: The students left the building in an orderly manner.
ex.ko: 학생들은 질서 있게 건물을 빠져나갔다.

ordinary.en: not special or different in any way; normal

ex.en: It was just an ordinary day at work.
ex.ko: 그저 평범한 직장 하루였다.

organ.en: a part of the body that has a particular function, such as the heart or lungs

ex.en: The heart is a vital organ.
ex.ko: 심장은 필수적인 장기이다.

organ.en: a large musical instrument with pipes and a keyboard, often played in churches

ex.en: She played the organ at the church wedding.
ex.ko: 그녀는 교회 결혼식에서 오르간을 연주했다.

organic.en: (of food) grown or produced without artificial chemicals

ex.en: They only buy organic vegetables.
ex.ko: 그들은 유기농 채소만 산다.

organic.en: relating to or coming from living things

ex.en: Fossil fuels come from organic matter.
ex.ko: 화석 연료는 유기물에서 나온다.

organism.en: a living thing, such as an animal, plant, or bacterium

ex.en: Bacteria are tiny organisms that can only be seen under a microscope.
ex.ko: 박테리아는 현미경으로만 볼 수 있는 아주 작은 생물이다.

organization.en: a group of people who work together for a particular purpose, such as a business or charity

ex.en: She works for a nonprofit organization.
ex.ko: 그녀는 비영리 단체에서 일한다.

organization.en: the way in which the parts of something are arranged or planned

ex.en: The event needed better organization.
ex.ko: 그 행사는 더 나은 체계가 필요했다.

origin.en: the point or place where something begins or comes from

ex.en: The origin of the tradition is unclear.
ex.ko: 그 전통의 기원은 명확하지 않다.

origin.en: the country, race, or social background that a person comes from

ex.en: She is of Italian origin.
ex.ko: 그녀는 이탈리아 출신이다.

ex.en: Wear a coat, otherwise you'll get cold.
ex.ko: 코트를 입어, 그렇지 않으면 추울 거야.

otherwise.en: apart from what has just been mentioned; in other respects

ex.en: The car is old but otherwise in great condition.
ex.ko: 그 차는 낡았지만 그 외에는 상태가 아주 좋다.

ought to.en: used to say what is the right or sensible thing to do

ex.en: You ought to apologize to her.
ex.ko: 너는 그녀에게 사과해야 한다.

out-of-date.en: no longer useful, valid, or fashionable because it is old

ex.en: This map is out-of-date; the roads have changed.
ex.ko: 이 지도는 오래돼서 도로가 바뀌었다.

outdoor.en: happening or used outside rather than in a building

ex.en: They enjoy outdoor activities like hiking.
ex.ko: 그들은 하이킹 같은 야외 활동을 즐긴다.

outdoors.en: outside rather than in a building

ex.en: The children love playing outdoors.
ex.ko: 아이들은 야외에서 노는 것을 좋아한다.

ex.en: Remove the outer layer of the onion.
ex.ko: 양파의 겉껍질을 벗겨라.

outlaw.en: a person who has broken the law and is hiding from the authorities

ex.en: The outlaw was finally captured after years on the run.
ex.ko: 그 무법자는 수년간 도피 끝에 결국 붙잡혔다.

ex.en: The country outlawed smoking in public places.
ex.ko: 그 나라는 공공장소에서 흡연을 금지했다.

outline.en: the line that shows the shape or edge of something

ex.en: You could see the outline of the mountains against the sky.
ex.ko: 하늘을 배경으로 산의 윤곽이 보였다.

outline.en: a short description of the main points of something

ex.en: She wrote an outline before starting the essay.
ex.ko: 그녀는 에세이를 쓰기 전에 개요를 작성했다.

outline.en: give the main points or a summary of something

ex.en: The manager outlined the new plan to the team.
ex.ko: 매니저는 팀에게 새 계획의 개요를 설명했다.

ex.en: She received an award for outstanding performance.
ex.ko: 그녀는 뛰어난 성과로 상을 받았다.

outstanding.cue: 아직 갚지 않은 돈·처리 안 된 일
outstanding.en: not yet paid, done, or dealt with

ex.en: There is still an outstanding balance on the loan.
ex.ko: 그 대출에는 아직 미지급 잔액이 있다.

outward.en: relating to how someone or something appears, rather than what is actually true inside

ex.en: Despite his outward calm, he was very nervous.
ex.ko: 겉으로는 침착해 보였지만 그는 매우 긴장하고 있었다.

outward.en: going away from a place, especially at the start of a journey

ex.en: The outward flight was delayed by two hours.
ex.ko: 출발편은 두 시간 지연되었다.

outweigh.en: be greater or more important than something else

ex.en: The benefits of the plan outweigh the risks.
ex.ko: 그 계획의 이점이 위험보다 더 크다.

overcome.en: successfully deal with or defeat a problem or difficulty

ex.en: She overcame many obstacles to achieve her dream.
ex.ko: 그녀는 꿈을 이루기 위해 많은 장애물을 극복했다.

overcome.en: be affected very strongly by a feeling

ex.en: He was overcome with joy when he saw his family.
ex.ko: 그는 가족을 보고 기쁨에 압도되었다.

ex.en: She was overjoyed to see her old friend again.
ex.ko: 그녀는 옛 친구를 다시 만나 매우 기뻐했다.

ex.en: We stayed overnight at a hotel near the airport.
ex.ko: 우리는 공항 근처 호텔에서 하룻밤 묵었다.

ex.en: The song became a hit overnight.
ex.ko: 그 노래는 하룻밤 사이에 히트곡이 되었다.

overwhelm.en: affect someone so strongly that they don't know how to react

ex.en: She was overwhelmed by the amount of support she received.
ex.ko: 그녀는 받은 지지의 양에 압도되었다.

overwhelm.en: give someone so much of something that they cannot deal with it

ex.en: The new employee felt overwhelmed by all the tasks.
ex.ko: 신입 사원은 밀려드는 모든 업무에 쩔쩔맸다.

overwhelming.en: so great or powerful that it is difficult to deal with or resist

ex.en: There was overwhelming support for the proposal.
ex.ko: 그 제안에 대한 지지가 압도적이었다.

overwork.en: the state of working too much or too hard

ex.en: He collapsed from overwork.
ex.ko: 그는 과로로 쓰러졌다.

overwork.cue: 몸이 상할 정도로 일을 너무 많이 하다
overwork.en: make someone work too hard, or work too hard yourself

ex.en: Don't overwork yourself before the exam.
ex.ko: 시험 전에 너무 무리해서 공부하지 마.

owe.en: have to pay money back to someone

ex.en: I still owe him twenty dollars.
ex.ko: 나는 아직 그에게 20달러를 빚지고 있다.

owe.en: feel grateful to someone for something they have done for you

ex.en: I owe my success to my parents' support.
ex.ko: 나의 성공은 부모님의 지원 덕분이다.

ex.en: There is a small amount owing on the bill.
ex.ko: 청구서에 소액이 아직 지불되지 않고 남아 있다.

owing.en: because of; as a result of (used in the phrase 'owing to')

ex.en: The flight was delayed owing to bad weather.
ex.ko: 그 항공편은 악천후로 인해 지연되었다.
